title: EVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CONSUMED — Budget envelope consumed (design-time documentation)
description: DESIGN-TIME DOCUMENTATION ONLY. This schema describes the abstract business fact 'a budget envelope has been partially or fully consumed within the period limits' at the meta-model level (business event, per ADR-BCM-URBA-0007 / ADR-BCM-URBA-0009). PER ADR-TECH-STRAT-001 RULE 2, NO AUTONOMOUS BUS MESSAGE CORRESPONDS TO THIS SCHEMA. Only the paired resource event RVT.BSP.004.CONSUMPTION_RECORDED generates wire-level messages on RabbitMQ; the business event appears only as the prefix of the routing key (Rule 4: 'EVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CONSUMED.RVT.BSP.004.CONSUMPTION_RECORDED'). This file exists for: (a) traceability of the BCM business-event meta-model into a machine-readable artefact, (b) governance review under ADR-BCM-URBA-0009, (c) consumer documentation describing the abstract fact independently of its wire-level projection. DO NOT publish payloads validated against this schema on the bus — the wire-level contract is RVT.BSP.004.CONSUMPTION_RECORDED.schema.json. CORRELATION KEY: 'case_id'. Resolution path to OBJ.REF.001.BENEFICIARY_RECORD.internal_id via CAP.REF.001.BEN is the consumer's concern. AVAILABLE BALANCE DERIVATION (consumer-side convenience): available_balance = cap_amount - consumed_amount; this is a derived value, NOT a transported field — consumers compute it from the OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION fields they already hold.

allocation_id.description: Unique identifier of the budget allocation (the inner envelope of AGG.BSP.004.ENV.PERIOD_BUDGET). Maps to OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.allocation_id.

case_id.description: CORRELATION KEY. Reference to the relevant participation case (owned by CAP.BSP.002.ENR). Resolution path to OBJ.REF.001.BENEFICIARY_RECORD.internal_id via CAP.REF.001.BEN is the consumer's concern.

category.description: Spending category of the envelope. Canonical category sets per tier are owned by CAP.REF.001.TIE.

cap_amount.description: Maximum amount authorised for the envelope in the period (OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.cap_amount). Defined by the beneficiary's current tier (CAP.REF.001.TIE).

consumed_amount.description: Amount actually consumed in the envelope at the time the business fact is observed (OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.consumed_amount). Available balance = cap_amount - consumed_amount (derived; not a separate transported field).

status.description: Envelope lifecycle status (OBJ.BSP.004.ALLOCATION.status). For EVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_CONSUMED, status is typically OPEN (envelope still active) or DEPLETED (cap reached by this consumption — paired with EVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_DEPLETED). UNCONSUMED is reserved for end-of-period non-consumption (paired with EVT.BSP.004.ENVELOPE_UNCONSUMED).
